definitions.text: Having little thickness or extent from one surface to its opposite.
examples: thin plate of metal; thin paper; thin board; thin covering

definitions.text: Very narrow in all diameters; having a cross section that is small in all directions.
Having little body fat or flesh; slim; slender; lean; gaunt.
A loss or tearing of paper from the back of a stamp, although not sufficient to create a complete hole.
Any food produced or served in thin slices.

examples: The crowds thinned after the procession had passed: there was nothing more to see.

definitions.text: Not thickly or closely; in a scattered state.
